question: How to ensure future startup commands do not recreate whop-auto-ytdlp-pot-provider-1?
safe_target_behavior: /opt/crypto-tuber-ranked/scripts/start-whop-auto-workers.sh --start, which executes docker compose -f /opt/crypto-tuber-ranked/docker-compose.yml -p whop-auto up -d --no-deps --no-recreate hermes-worker channel-agent-worker after singleton health checks
why_no_deps_required: The shared compose file has depends_on: ytdlp-pot-provider for whop-auto worker services; explicit service names without --no-deps can still recreate the provider dependency under whop-auto.

routes.evidence: ExecStart=/usr/bin/docker compose up -d hermes-worker in /opt/crypto-tuber-ranked; no -p whop-auto. It belongs to crypto project and does not start whop-auto.
No whop-auto Docker Compose startup found in captured systemd unit refs.
No whop-auto Docker Compose startup found in captured cron refs.
cronjob list showed CallScore jobs/scripts/workdirs but no whop-auto Docker Compose startup route; filesystem scan under /srv/agents/hermes/scripts found no docker compose whop-auto start command.
Created wrapper uses --no-deps, --no-recreate, and explicit hermes-worker channel-agent-worker service targets after singleton health and duplicate absence checks.

routes.route: manual operator command: docker compose -p whop-auto up -d
routes.evidence: Would materialize ytdlp-pot-provider under whop-auto because base compose defines the service and workers depend_on it.

routes.route: manual operator command: docker compose -p whop-auto up -d hermes-worker channel-agent-worker without --no-deps
routes.evidence: Explicit services alone are insufficient because compose dependency resolution may start ytdlp-pot-provider.
No package script starts whop-auto Docker Compose services.

Historical docs contain many stale mentions of whop-auto and compose; canonical new runbook is docs/ops/ytdlp-singleton-provider.md and clean-kitchen deliverables. Do not use archived snippets as startup authority.
